topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The scent of old paper and something vaguely floral hung heavy in the air of the shared room. I was halfway through sketching in my journal, the graphite scratching rhythmically against the page, when I noticed the door ajar. My heart gave a sudden, sharp thump against my ribs. Peeking over the edge of the desk, I saw a familiar silhouette—Liam, my roommate, his head bent close to my open book. A cold prickle traced its way down my spine. I froze, the pencil hovering mid-stroke. He didn't move, just continued to read, his breathing shallow and almost imperceptible. Slowly, agonizingly, I lowered my gaze, trying to look absorbed in my own work, but my fingers were suddenly clammy. When he finally looked up, his eyes met mine for a fleeting second, and a flicker of something unreadable crossed his face before he quickly returned to his page. I quickly snapped the journal shut, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet room, and retreated to my bed, meticulously smoothing down the covers, my gaze fixed on the door.

[story 2]

It started subtly. I’d left my journal open on my desk one afternoon, a half-finished poem staring back at me. When I returned an hour later, the pages were slightly askew. I picked it up, my fingers brushing against a passage I hadn't written yet—a raw confession about my anxieties. A wave of tension washed over me, making my palms sweat. Later that evening, I heard a soft rustling sound coming from Liam’s side of the room. I waited, holding my breath until the sound ceased. When I finally gathered the courage to approach him, I found him sitting on his bed, a book open in his lap, his expression placid. "Everything alright?" I managed, my voice sounding thin. He looked up, a slight, almost apologetic smile on his lips. "Just catching up on some reading," he said, his tone perfectly even. But as he shifted, I saw the spine of my journal tucked discreetly beneath his textbook. The air in the room felt suddenly thick, pressing in on my chest.

[story 3]

The silence in the dorm room had become oppressive lately, punctuated only by the distant hum of the ventilation system. I was trying to focus on a particularly difficult essay, but my attention kept drifting to Liam. I found myself watching him from across the room, a strange, tight feeling coiling in my stomach. Later, I decided to check on my belongings. My journal was gone from its usual spot. Panic began to bubble up, a frantic need to find it. I searched frantically—under the bed, inside my backpack—but it wasn't there. Then, I saw it: lying face-up on Liam’s desk, open to a page detailing my most private thoughts. My breath hitched. I felt a sudden, profound sense of exposure, as if every secret I’d ever held was now laid bare for inspection. I didn't confront him immediately. Instead, I backed away slowly, my movements stiff, the heat rising in my cheeks, forcing me to retreat to the bathroom, counting the slow, deliberate breaths I needed to regain control.
